question: Additive optional 'agent' field on TrajectoryStep (schema v2→v3, the 'command' precedent): approve? v2 records read back null agent throughout; no reader may require the field.
author: claude/design

question: Generic normalized log format v2 (agent on steps + telemetry_by_model keyed strictly by the EVAL-13 declared model set): approve with the v1-parses-forever compatibility posture?
author: claude/design

question: Agent label identity posture. Free labels through the single scrub have a demonstrated leak vector: arm canaries are exact-match full-id literals, so a label embedding a model-name FRAGMENT ('llama-planner') matches neither the built-in patterns (no llama/qwen/deepseek/mistral entries) nor the exact literals, and leaks on published surfaces. Alternatives: pre-registered label roster (identity-scanned at lock; breaks dynamically-spawned agents), pseudonymize at capture (kills the leak and the legibility), or raw-in-artifact + pseudonymized-at-publish (per-trial deterministic label→ordinal mapping applied by the blinding wrapper on packet/dossier surfaces only; forensics reads raw). Genuine fork.
author: claude/design

question: Build gating: hold EVAL-14 until EVAL-13 lands (declared model set is the by-model validation universe) with AC-5's exploratory per-model section as the named consumer that unblocks building, or build both stories concurrently?
author: claude/design

question: AMENDED after 2026-07-04 constraint (do not touch the blind subsystem, which rules out raw-artifact-pseudonymized-publish; inference at intake rejected as estimation — the harness must supply identity, so there is nothing to infer from). New option: closed-role-vocabulary — labels match role(-ordinal)? over a closed role enum published in the format standard (planner, executor, orchestrator, router, critic, reviewer, tester, researcher, worker), validated loudly at the generic-adapter parse; identity leakage is unrepresentable by construction; vocabulary extension requires a format-version bump. Costs: bounded expressiveness (worker-N catch-all); fleet size/shape remains a stylistic tell on advisory surfaces.
author: claude/design

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: strong recommendations accepted. Additive optional 'agent' on TrajectoryStep (v2→v3, the 'command' precedent); v2 records read back null throughout; no reader may require the field.
migration: absent field = pre-EVAL-14 record; never refuse legacy artifacts
author: jyang

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: strong recommendations accepted. verdi_log_version 2 carries telemetry_by_model keyed strictly by the EVAL-13 declared set; v1 logs parse unchanged forever. Noted coupling: once TrajectoryStep v3 lands, 'agent' validates in any declared generic log regardless of log version — the log-version bump is load-bearing for telemetry_by_model.
migration: v1-parses-forever; v2 keys validated against the locked declared model set
author: jyang

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: strong recommendations accepted, after the constraint that the blind subsystem must not change. Labels match role(-ordinal)? over the closed role enum published in the format standard, validated loudly at the generic-adapter parse; identity leakage unrepresentable by construction; vocabulary extension requires a format-version bump.
migration: n/a — new surface; no existing records carry labels
author: jyang

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: strong recommendations accepted. EVAL-14 builds after EVAL-13 lands; AC-5's exploratory per-model report section is the named consumer that unblocks building.
author: jyang
